title: eriogonum umbellatum var. aureum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. aureum, also known as the golden sulfur buckwheat, is a variety of the sulfur buckwheat plant that is native to the western United States. It is a shrubby perennial herb with clusters of small, golden flowers that have been used in herbal medicine for centuries. The golden sulfur buckwheat has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
